pr.title: fix(webhook): sanitize WEBHOOK_SECRET into URL-safe form
pr.body:
## Summary

After deploying to Render the bot was not receiving any updates from Telegram. `getWebhookInfo` returned a webhook URL of `https://vpkn-bot.onrender.com/telegram//OlRENLtjek2gSxJzLQROdbxTR6xEaUxmCujmtirkzE=` — note the **double slash** and the trailing `=`.

Root cause: Render's `generateValue: true` for `WEBHOOK_SECRET` produces standard base64 output that frequently contains `+`, `/` and `=`. Two failure modes from that:

1. A leading `/` in the secret produced `…/telegram//<rest>` in the URL. Telegram's edge / Render's reverse proxy normalises double slashes, so the path Telegram actually POSTed to didn't match the path PTB was listening on.
2. Telegram's `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` header only accepts `[A-Za-z0-9_-]`. A secret containing `/` or `+` would fail validation, so the previous defensive `isalnum()` / character-set check silently set `secret_token=None` — meaning we got no header validation either.

This PR adds `_sanitize_secret()` which converts whatever is in `WEBHOOK_SECRET` into base64url form (`+` → `-`, `/` → `_`, `=` stripped, everything else stripped just in case) and uses that sanitised value as both the URL path component **and** the `secret_token` header. If after sanitisation the token is shorter than 16 chars, we regenerate a fresh `secrets.token_urlsafe(32)` to guarantee we never run with a too-short secret.

Also adds `drop_pending_updates=True` on the webhook path to discard the queue of failed delivery attempts that built up while the webhook was broken — otherwise the first successful boot would replay them all at once.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Merge this PR, wait for Render to auto-redeploy (~5 min Docker build).
- [ ] In Render → service `vpkn-bot` → **Logs**: look for a line starting with `Starting webhook at https://vpkn-bot.onrender.com/telegram/…` and confirm the path **does not** contain a double slash or trailing `=`.
- [ ] In Telegram, message [@vpkn_40733511_bot](https://t.me/vpkn_40733511_bot) `/start` — the bot should answer. Then test free-form chat, `/pdf`, `/flyer`, `/image`.

### Notes

- Verified the sanitizer locally on the Render-style secret returned by `getWebhookInfo` (`/OlR…=`) — it produces `_OlR…` (43 chars, all URL-safe).
- An alternative would have been to override `WEBHOOK_SECRET` in the Render dashboard to a known-good value, but a defensive sanitizer protects against any future regenerate or copy-paste containing forbidden chars.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/6091e9aa95da47f98f6a0e25aa82625f
Requested by: @infomaximkeller90-byte
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
